en.extract:
The New Britain bronzewing (Henicophaps foersteri) is a species of bird in the family Columbidae. It is endemic to Papua New Guinea. In 1988, it was rated as a near threatened species on the International Union for Conservation of Nature Red List of Endangered Species. However, in 2000, it was warranted a vulnerable status.


Description
The New Britain bronzewing measures about 38 centimetres (15 in) in length, having 20.5 centimetres (8.1 in) long wingspan. The tail measures 13.7 centimetres (5.4 in), and the bill measures 3 centimetres (1.2 in) in length. It has a buff coloured forehead and a reddish brown crown. The irides and the bill are dark. It has dark brown upperparts, and the chin, throat, and ear feathers are creamy white. The greater and the median coverts are metallic golden green, and the lesser coverts are dark maroon. It has blue iridescent tertials.
It emits a monotonously repeating pip-yia call, with the second note being higher pitched than the first note.


Distribution and habitat
The New Britain bronzewing is endemic to Papua New Guinea. Its natural habitat is old-growth and second-growth lowland forests up to elevations of at least 700 metres (2,300 ft) above sea level.


Status and conservation
In 1988, the New Britain bronzewing was rated as a near threatened species on the IUCN Red List of Endangered Species. However, in 2000, it was warranted a vulnerable status due to its small population numbering less than 5,000, which is probably decreasing due to habitat loss. It is a rare species. It has been recorded in the Poikili and the Garu Wildlife Protection Areas.


References


External links

BirdLife Species Factsheet.
"New Britain Btonzewing" (Audio) (Recording). Xeno canto.

The '''New Britain bronzewing''' ('''''Henicophaps foersteri''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to [[Papua New Guinea]]. In 1988, it was rated as a [[Near-threatened species|near threatened]] species on the [[IUCN Red List of Endangered Species|International Union for Conservation of Nature Red List of Endangered Species]]. However, in 2000, it was warranted a [[Vulnerable species|vulnerable]] status.

== Description ==
The New Britain bronzewing measures about {{Convert|38|cm|in}} in length,<ref name="hbw2" /> having {{Convert|20.5|cm|in}} long wingspan. The tail measures {{Convert|13.7|cm|in}}, and the bill measures {{Convert|3|cm|in}} in length.<ref name=":0">{{Cite book|title=Pigeons and Doves: A Guide to the Pigeons and Doves of the World|last1=Gibbs|first1=David|author-link1=David Gibbs (naturalist)|date=2010|publisher=[[A & C Black|A&C Black]]|location=London |isbn=978-1-4081-3556-3|language=en|url=https://books.google.com/books?id=r__Tx8QKQfMC&pg=PA298 |pages=298–299}}</ref> It has a buff coloured forehead and a reddish brown [[Crown (anatomy)|crown]].<ref name="hbw2">{{cite web|url=http://www.hbw.com/species/new-britain-bronzewing-henicophaps-foersteri|title=New Britain Bronzewing (''Henicophaps foersteri'')|last1=Baptista|first1=L.F.|last2=Trail|first2=P.W.|year=2017|editor1-last=del Hoyo|editor1-first=Josep|editor2-last=Elliott|editor2-first=Andrew|work=Handbook of the Birds of the World Alive|publisher=Lynx Edicions|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170712212303/http://www.hbw.com/species/new-britain-bronzewing-henicophaps-foersteri|archive-date=2017-07-12|access-date=2017-09-19|url-access=subscription |last3=Horblit|first3=H.M.|editor3-last=Sargatal|editor3-first=Jordi|editor4-last=Christie|editor4-first=David A|editor5-last=de Juana|editor5-first=Eduardo}}</ref> The irides and the [[Beak|bill]] are dark. It has dark brown [[upperparts]], and the chin, throat, and ear feathers are creamy white. The greater and the median coverts are metallic golden green, and the lesser coverts are dark maroon. It has blue iridescent [[tertials]].<ref name=":0" />

It emits a monotonously repeating ''pip-yia'' call, with the second note being higher pitched than the first note.<ref name=":0" />

== Distribution and habitat ==
The New Britain bronzewing is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Papua New Guinea]]. Its natural habitat is [[Old-growth forest|old-growth]] and [[second-growth]] lowland [[forest]]s up to elevations of at least {{Convert|700|m|ft}} above sea level.<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021" /><ref name="Beehler">{{cite book |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=wRR1CwAAQBAJ&pg=PA68 |isbn=978-1-4008-8071-3 |first1=Bruce M. |last1=Beehler |author-link1=Bruce Beehler|first2=Thane K. |last2=Pratt |first3=Mary |last3=Lecroy |location=Princeton, New Jersey |publisher=[[Princeton University Press]] |year=2016|title=Birds of New Guinea: Distribution, Taxonomy, and Systematics |pages=38–69}}</ref>

== Status and conservation ==
In 1988, the New Britain bronzewing was rated as a [[Near-threatened species|near threatened]] species on the [[IUCN Red List of Endangered Species]]. However, in 2000, it was warranted a [[Vulnerable species|vulnerable]] status due to its small population numbering less than 5,000, which is probably decreasing due to habitat loss. It is a rare species. It has been recorded in the Poikili and the Garu Wildlife Protection Areas.<ref name="iucn status 13 November 2021" />
